name: gcp-create-push-subscription
description:
Create a Google Cloud Pub/Sub PUSH subscription with OIDC-authenticated delivery via 'PUT https://pubsub.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{PROJECT_ID}/subscriptions/{SUBSCRIPTION_NAME}'. Binds the subscription to a topic and points push delivery at a Talos watch-channel endpoint, with an OIDC token minted by the given service account against the given audience (ackDeadlineSeconds=60). Idempotent: if the subscription already exists (HTTP 409) the module returns SUCCESS with already_existed=true, so re-running a provisioning workflow is safe. Second of the three Phase C provisioning modules — the self-serve automation of docs/gcp-push-setup.md (topic → push subscription → monitoring channel).

This is a WRITE-capable module: it requires the write-tier Google Cloud OAuth consent granted via '/api/gcp/connect-write' (a SEPARATE consent from the read-only '/api/gcp/connect' flow). Set AUTH_HEADER to a vault:// reference under 'oauth/google_cloud_write/...' — the controller resolves + refreshes the token at dispatch time.

AUTH_HEADER.description: vault:// path to the WRITE-tier OAuth access token wrapped in 'Bearer ' (e.g. 'Bearer vault://oauth/google_cloud_write/{user_id}/{provider_key}/access_token'). The controller resolves the vault reference and refreshes the token if expired. Note the '_write' namespace — the read-tier 'oauth/google_cloud/*' token cannot create subscriptions.

PROJECT_ID.description: GCP project id that owns the topic and the new subscription. Must match ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]{4,28}[a-z0-9]$ (validated before the request is built).

TOPIC_NAME.description: Short name of the EXISTING topic to subscribe to (create it first with 'GCP: Create Pub/Sub Topic'). Must match ^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9._~+%-]{2,254}$ and must not start with 'goog'.

SUBSCRIPTION_NAME.description: Short subscription name (NOT the full 'projects/...' path). Must match ^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9._~+%-]{2,254}$ and must not start with 'goog' (validated before the request is built).

PUSH_ENDPOINT.description: The Talos push URL for this watch channel — copy it from the watch-channel row (Settings → Google Cloud Watch Channels). Must start with https:// (Pub/Sub push requires HTTPS).

SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL.description: Service account that mints the push OIDC token. MUST equal the watch's expected service account (see docs/gcp-push-setup.md) — Talos rejects pushes signed by any other SA. Shape: <name>@<project>.iam.gserviceaccount.com.

AUDIENCE.description: OIDC token audience. MUST equal the Talos controller's GCP_PUBSUB_AUDIENCE env value — an audience mismatch makes Talos reject every push with 401. Non-empty, no whitespace.

setup_instructions.title: Create the topic first
setup_instructions.detail: Run 'GCP: Create Pub/Sub Topic' (or create the topic manually). The subscription PUT fails with 404 if the topic does not exist.

setup_instructions.title: Grant write-tier consent
setup_instructions.detail: Complete the WRITE-tier OAuth flow via '/api/gcp/connect-write' and set AUTH_HEADER to 'Bearer vault://oauth/google_cloud_write/{your_user_id}/{your_provider_key}/access_token'.

setup_instructions.title: Copy the push endpoint from Talos
setup_instructions.detail: The PUSH_ENDPOINT comes from the Talos watch-channel row (Settings → Google Cloud Watch Channels) — it embeds the per-watch URL token that authenticates the channel.

setup_instructions.title: Match audience and service account to the controller
setup_instructions.detail: AUDIENCE must equal the controller's GCP_PUBSUB_AUDIENCE, and SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL must equal the watch's expected service account (see docs/gcp-push-setup.md). Mismatches surface as 401-rejected pushes, not as errors from this module.

setup_instructions.title: Finish with the monitoring channel
setup_instructions.detail: Run 'GCP: Create Monitoring Channel' to point Cloud Monitoring incident notifications at the topic. Re-runs of this module are safe — an existing subscription returns already_existed=true instead of failing.
